page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-focused server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Storage is a key component of this configuration, which bundles 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across four drives. The x86_64 architecture includes specialized CPU instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

Benchmark results show balanced performance across compute, memory, and storage workloads. The instance achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,066.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 37,463.33. Its memory subsystem delivers a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 2,175.06 GB/sec and a large-block uncached read bandwidth of 115.28 GB/sec. In data processing tasks, the server demonstrates strong capabilities, particularly in image processing with a Geekbench score of 14,126.0 and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression at 171.82 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds reach 2,433.87 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a small 135M model, though performance scales down to 17.84 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the z3-highmem-32-highlssd offers high resource density by combining a substantial memory footprint with massive local NVMe SSD storage on a single dedicated-core instance. This eliminates the need for separate high-performance network storage attachments for IOPS-heavy tasks. The hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, local caching layers, and applications requiring high-throughput local storage. It is also capable of handling moderate CPU-based machine learning inference and image processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-focused server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 support. Benchmark data indicates balanced performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 37,463.33, a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,066.0, and strong image processing capabilities. The server delivers a cached memory read bandwidth of 2,175.06 GB/sec and handles 35.61 million Redis SET operations per second. This high-density configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency by bundling massive local storage with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is designed for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput data processing, and local caching workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 12,884 GB local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and massive local NVMe SSD capacity for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors.
12,884 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled.
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction support.
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM inference.
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and high-throughput data processing.
